instructions.name: Maestro Scan and Plan
instructions.objective: Scan the entire project, analyze gaps, assign agents to work, build per-agent memory files, and produce a master battle plan

llm.mandate: This workflow runs in YOLO mode by default — no user confirmation between steps
Read ALL files completely — never use offset/limit
Every agent assignment must be justified by evidence from the scan
Memory files must contain actionable context, not generic summaries

step.goal: Ensure context is fresh and gather all project intelligence
step.action: Display: "⚔️ PHASE 1: RECONNAISSANCE — Scanning the battlefield..."
Check if {project-root}/scripts/context/generate_all.py exists

check.if: context files are stale or missing

step.action: Read {project-root}/output/project-context.md (if exists) — store as {project_context_content}
Read {project-root}/output/context/module-index.md (if exists) — store as {module_index_content}
Read {project-root}/output/context/api-index.md (if exists) — store as {api_index_content}
Read {project-root}/output/context/schema-digest.md (if exists) — store as {schema_digest_content}
Read {project-root}/output/context/patterns.md (if exists) — store as {patterns_content}
Read {project-root}/output/context/sprint-digest.md (if exists) — store as {sprint_digest_content}
Read {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml — store as {sprint_status_content}
Scan the top-level project directory structure using ls to understand the codebase layout
If src/ or app/ or lib/ directories exist, scan their structure one level deep
Read {project-root}/team/agent-manifest.csv — store as {agent_roster}
Read {project-root}/team/manifest.yaml — store as {team_definitions}
Check {project-root}/team/_memory/ for any existing agent memory files
Check if {project-root}/.agents/registry/ exists and contains agent files

check.action: Display: "Multi-agent coordination detected. {N} active agents, {M} active claims."
If orphaned agents found, display: "⚠️ {K} orphaned agent worktrees need review."

step.action: Display summary: "Recon complete. [X] context files loaded, [Y] planning artifacts found, [Z] stories tracked."

step.goal: Analyze everything found and categorize work needed
step.action: Display: "⚔️ PHASE 2: ANALYSIS — Identifying targets..."

Analyze all gathered intelligence and categorize findings into these work domains:
DOMAIN CATEGORIES: ┌─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Domain │ What to look for │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ ARCHITECTURE │ Missing architecture docs, unresolved design │ │ │ decisions, scalability concerns, tech debt patterns │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ IMPLEMENTATION │ Stories in backlog/ready-for-dev, features planned │ │ │ but not started, incomplete implementations │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ TESTING │ Missing test coverage, stories without tests, │ │ │ test architecture gaps, CI/CD test pipeline gaps │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ SECURITY │ Missing security reviews, OWASP concerns, │ │ │ authentication/authorization gaps, data exposure │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ DOCUMENTATION │ Missing docs, stale docs, API docs, user guides │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ DEVOPS │ CI/CD gaps, deployment concerns, environment issues, │ │ │ Docker/container needs, monitoring gaps │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ DATA │ Schema gaps, migration needs, query optimization, │ │ │ caching strategy, data modeling issues │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ API │ Contract drift, missing endpoints, versioning issues, │ │ │ OpenAPI spec gaps │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ COMPLIANCE │ NIST RMF gaps, HIPAA concerns, regulatory needs, │ │ │ industry-specific requirements │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ UX │ Missing UX designs, user flow gaps, accessibility, │ │ │ usability concerns │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ PRODUCT │ Missing PRD sections, unclear requirements, │ │ │ stakeholder alignment gaps │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ AI/ML │ Model architecture needs, training pipeline gaps, │ │ │ evaluation methodology, MLOps needs │ ├─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ CREATIVE │ Branding needs, presentation needs, storytelling gaps │ └─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
For each domain, produce: - FINDINGS: What specifically was found (cite files, line numbers, artifact names) - PRIORITY: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW - WORK ITEMS: Specific tasks that need doing - DEPENDENCIES: What must happen first
Skip any domain where no findings exist — don't pad the report.
Display the gap analysis as a clean table: "TARGET ACQUISITION: | # | Domain | Priority | Findings | Work Items | |---|--------|----------|----------|------------| "

step.goal: Map each work item to the best agent(s) from the roster
step.action: Display: "⚔️ PHASE 3: SQUAD ASSIGNMENT — Deploying the team..."

Using the gap analysis from Phase 2 and the agent roster from agent-manifest.csv, assign agents to work items:
ASSIGNMENT RULES: 1. Match work domains to agent expertise: - ARCHITECTURE → Winston (Architect) - IMPLEMENTATION → Amelia (Dev) for coding, Athena/Maestro for lifecycle - TESTING → Murat (Tea - Test Architect) - SECURITY → Shield (Security Auditor) - DOCUMENTATION → Paige (Tech Writer) - DEVOPS → Forge (DevOps) - DATA → Oracle (Data Architect) - API → Pact (API Contract) - COMPLIANCE → Atlas (NIST RMF), Dr. Vita (Healthcare), Senator (Government), Sterling (Financial) — as applicable - UX → Sally (UX Designer) - PRODUCT → John (PM), Mary (Analyst) - AI/ML → Nexus (Agentic Expert), Neuron (ML Expert) — as applicable - CREATIVE → Carson (Creative Coach), Maya (Design Coach), Sophia (Storyteller) — as applicable
2. Each work item gets a PRIMARY agent (does the work) and optional SUPPORT agents (consult) 3. Order assignments by priority: CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW 4. Identify PARALLEL work (items with no dependencies on each other) 5. Identify SEQUENTIAL work (items that must happen in order) 6. Flag any items that need TEAM coordination (multiple agents working together)

check.action: For each story being assigned: check if it is already claimed in {active_claims}
If claimed: skip assignment, note "Already claimed by agent {id}"
If not claimed: proceed with normal assignment
Include active agent summary in the battle plan output

step.action:
Display agent assignments: "SQUAD DEPLOYMENT: | Agent | Role | Assignments | Priority | Invoke | |-------|------|-------------|----------|--------|
EXECUTION ORDER: Phase 1 (Parallel): [agents that can work simultaneously] Phase 2 (Sequential): [agents that depend on Phase 1] Phase 3 (Sequential): [agents that depend on Phase 2] ... "

step.goal: Create mission briefing (memory) files for each assigned agent
step.action: Display: "⚔️ PHASE 4: MISSION BRIEFINGS — Arming the squad..."

For EACH agent that received assignments in Phase 3, create a memory file at: {project-root}/team/_memory/{agent-name}/mission.md
Create the directory {project-root}/team/_memory/{agent-name}/ if it doesn't exist.
Each mission.md file MUST contain:
```markdown --- agent: {agent-display-name} ({agent-name}) generated: {date} generated_by: Maestro scan-and-plan priority: {highest priority of their assignments} ---
# Mission Briefing: {agent-display-name}
## Project Context [2-3 sentences about the project relevant to THIS agent's domain] [Key technologies, patterns, or constraints they need to know]
## Your Assignments [Numbered list of specific work items assigned to this agent] [Each item includes: description, priority, acceptance criteria, dependencies]
### Assignment 1: [title] - **Priority:** [CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW] - **Description:** [What needs to be done] - **Key Files:** [Specific files/directories relevant to this work] - **Acceptance Criteria:** [How we know it's done] - **Dependencies:** [What must happen first, or "None"] - **Invoke:** [The slash command to start, e.g., /team:architect]
## Key Files and Directories [List of specific files this agent needs to read/modify]
## Constraints [Project-specific constraints relevant to this agent] [Patterns they must follow from project-context.md or patterns.md]
## Coordination [Which other agents they need to coordinate with] [What they produce that other agents consume] ```
CRITICAL: Memory files must contain SPECIFIC, ACTIONABLE content from the scan. NOT "review the architecture" but "the API layer in src/routes/ lacks input validation on 3 endpoints: /users, /auth, /data"

Also create/update the Maestro memory file at {project-root}/team/_memory/maestro/latest-scan.md: ```markdown --- scan_date: {date} agents_deployed: [list of agent names] total_work_items: [count] critical_items: [count] ---
# Latest Scan Results [Summary of findings, assignments, and plan] [This file is read on next activation for continuity] ```
Display: "Mission briefings created for [N] agents. Memory files saved to team/_memory/"

step.goal: Generate and save the master battle plan
step.action: Display: "⚔️ PHASE 5: BATTLE PLAN — Assembling the master plan..."
step.template-output: Generate the master plan using the template, filling every section with findings from Phases 1-4. Save to {planning_artifacts}/master-plan.md

step.goal: Present the plan summary and await user direction
step.action:
Display the executive summary:
"⚔️ PLAN READY | {date} ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
SCOPE: [X] work items across [Y] domains SQUAD: [Z] agents deployed with mission briefings TIMELINE: [N] execution phases
TOP 3 PRIORITIES: 1. [Highest priority item] → [Agent] via [command] 2. [Second priority item] → [Agent] via [command] 3. [Third priority item] → [Agent] via [command]
RECOMMENDED FIRST MOVE: [specific action]
Master plan saved to: {planning_artifacts}/master-plan.md Agent memories saved to: team/_memory/ ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Your call, {user_name}. Pick a target or say NA for next action."
